title: Global Grain Trade Forecast Lowered
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) has released their latest global grain export forecast for the upcoming 2022/23 season and has reduced its expectations. The trade forecast is still very preliminary, but of note is that 488.9 million tons of exports are now expected. This is 2.9 million tons (-1%) less than was forecast a month ago and would mark a year-on-year decline of 20.5 million tons (-4%).
